div1.entryFree:
Dilate, vb. 1) to expand, to enlarge; part. --d == expansive: thy wisdom which, like a bourn, a pale, a shore, confines thy spacious and --d parts, Troil. II, 3, 261.
2) to relate at large, to enlarge upon: d. at full what hath befallen of them, Err. I, 1, 123. that I would all my pilgrimage d. Oth. I, 3, 153.
Dilated == detailed, copious, particular: take a more --d farewell, All's II, 1, 59. more than the scope of these --d articles allow, Hml. I, 2, 38 (Qq delated).
